title: silene atropurpurea
intro: Silene atropurpurea, commonly known as 'Atropurpurea Catchfly', is a flowering plant species within the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its dark red to purple flowers and hairy, glandular leaves. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which may aid in relieving coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. Silene atropurpurea's therapeutic actions are attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's bioactive constituents may also contribute to its potential use in treating various health conditions, including hypertension, diabetes, and skin disorders.
benefits: Silene atropurpurea has many benefits, such as its ability to improve respiratory health by reducing inflammation and congestion, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of asthma and other respiratory conditions. Additionally, it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, improve wound healing, and even lower blood pressure. The plant's antioxidant properties can also help to protect against cell damage, improve cognitive function, and even slow down the aging process, resulting in a healthier and more youthful appearance. By incorporating Silene atropurpurea into one's life, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy, and a greater sense of well-being, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
actions: Silene atropurpurea has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The plant's therapeutic actions also include antispasmodic and antihistaminic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies and asthma, improving breathing and reducing discomfort. Additionally, its antiseptic and astringent properties can aid in wound healing and prevent infections, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications. By harnessing these therapeutic actions, individuals can experience improved overall health, enhanced immune function, and increased quality of life.
intro_study: According to "Plant, cell & environment", Silene atropurpurea benefits from increased cytokinin biosynthesis due to high silicon intake, which enables it to tolerate various biotic and abiotic stresses and senesce more slowly.
constituents: Silene atropurpurea has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, phenolic acids like chlorogenic acid and ferulic acid, and triterpenoids like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These compounds have been found to improve people's daily lives by reducing inflammation and pain, protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage, and preventing the growth of harmful microorganisms. Additionally, the plant's constituents have been shown to possess immunomodulatory effects, supporting the immune system and enhancing its ability to fight off infections. By utilizing the medicinal properties of Silene atropurpurea, individuals can experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to enhanced quality of life.
parts: Silene atropurpurea has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, leaves, and roots. The flowers of Silene atropurpurea are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the plant contain alkaloids, including atropine and scopolamine, which have been used to treat various conditions, including glaucoma and gastrointestinal issues. The roots of Silene atropurpurea contain glycosides, including sileneoside and atropurpureaside, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations: Silene atropurpurea has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for various medicinal purposes. The tea made from the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. A tincture of Silene atropurpurea is used to treat inflammation and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Infusions of the plant are also used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Silene atropurpurea can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing excessive sweating, rapid heartbeat, and increased blood pressure due to its high levels of alkaloids. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to hallucinations, confusion, and altered mental states. Additionally, improper use of Silene atropurpurea may trigger allergic reactions, such as hives or itching, in some individuals. In severe cases, excessive consumption of the plant can even lead to seizures or respiratory failure.
